name: Fan-Out Wafer-Level Packaging
description: Wafer-level package architecture that embeds known-good dies in a reconstituted wafer or panel and fans I/O outward through redistribution layers.

dependencyEdges.note: Fan-out wafer-level packaging embeds and reconnects known-good integrated-circuit dies; the packaged die is the component being integrated.
Fan-out packages use patterned redistribution layers; photolithography is an enabling process for those fine package-level interconnects.

name: Hybrid Bonding Advanced Packaging
description: High-density die-to-die or wafer-to-wafer integration using direct bonding to create fine-pitch vertical interconnects for 3D chip stacks.

dependencyEdges.note: Hybrid bonding advanced packaging directly joins integrated-circuit dies or wafers into dense 3D chip stacks.
Hybrid bonding depends on very flat, clean bonding surfaces; planarization is an enabling semiconductor process for those surfaces.
High-density direct bonding is highly contamination-sensitive, so clean-room process control is a scaling dependency for reliable manufacturing.
